scope_note: QA I Ching Hexagram Orbit Cert [286]. Primary sources: (1) Iverson, B. (n.d.). 'Eight Keynotes.' www.svpvril.com/svpweb39.html. Accessed 2026-05-30. (2) Wilhelm, R. (trans. Baynes, C.F.) (1950). The I Ching or Book of Changes. Princeton University Press. ISBN 0-691-09750-X. Mechanism: QA Orbit Access Theorem cert [279]; extends QA I Ching Trigram Orbit cert [285]. CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): For hexagram code = lower + 8*upper (lower, upper in {0,...,7}, trigram 3-bit encoding from cert [285]): (a) code=0 (Kun-Kun) is A1-excluded; (b) code divisible by 9 iff lower=upper (doubled trigram) -- exactly 7 hexagrams: codes {9,18,27,36,45,54,63} = {Zhen-Zhen, Kan-Kan, Dui-Dui, Gen-Gen, Li-Li, Xun-Xun, Qian-Qian}; (c) code divisible by 3 but not 9 iff lower equiv upper mod 3, lower != upper -- exactly 14 hexagrams with Satellite access; (d) code coprime to 3 iff lower not-equiv upper mod 3 -- exactly 42 hexagrams, Cosmos-only. Partition: 1+7+14+42=64. Algebraic mechanism: 8 equiv -1 mod 9, so code mod 9 = (lower-upper) mod 9; Singularity iff lower-upper equiv 0 mod 9 iff lower=upper (since both < 8 < 9). Theorem NT compliance: integer arithmetic; no float feedback. Scope: does NOT certify King Wen sequence positions (only natural binary codes); does NOT claim divination interpretation; does NOT certify hexagram codes beyond {0,...,63}.
